prs.title: feat: add Dark/Light mode toggle (#800)
prs.body: This PR implements the Dark/Light mode toggle feature requested in #800 , allowing users to switch themes for improved accessibility and comfort. Problem: The application currently lacks a theme toggle. Users cannot switch between dark and light modes, limiting personalization and causing potential discomfort in low-light or high-light environments. Users who prefer dark mode for accessibility, eye strain reduction, or night-time use cannot apply their preference. Solution Implemented: ThemeToggle Component: Switches instantly between dark and light modes. Stores user preference in localStorage. Navbar Integration: Toggle added to both desktop and mobile navbars. Navbar links and menu

prs.title: [CLEANUP] Remove Debug Logs, Outdated TODOs, and Unused Props (#754)
prs.body:
This pull request addresses Issue #754 by performing a set of non-functional cleanup tasks aimed at improving code readability, maintainability, and consistency across the codebase.
No behavioral or feature changes were introduced.

Summary of Changes
1. Removed unnecessary debug logging

Cleaned up leftover debugging statements to reduce noise in production code:

Workspace.jsx – replaced console.log(error) with console.error(error)

db.js – removed console.log(e)

ControlPanel.jsx – removed console.log(error)

CodeEditor/index.jsx – removed console.log(e)

Modal.jsx – removed console.log(error)

This ensures proper error logging while maintaining cleaner output.

2. Removed outdated TODO comments

Deleted TODOs that were either irrelevant or already obsolete:

TypeInfo.jsx – removed legacy TODO related to index cleanup

dbml.js – removed outdated TODO while keeping the required timestamptz mapping logic intact

3. Cleaned up unused props

Since data.id is always available, some props and references were unnecessary:

TypeInfo.jsx

Removed unused index prop

Updated internal references to use data.id consistently

Updated itemKey to use data.id

TypesTab.jsx

Removed passing of the index prop to <TypeInfo />

Verification

Diagrams load and render correctly

Types tab functions as expected

DBML export behavior remains unchanged

Error logging is now more consistent and intentional

Conclusion

This cleanup improves overall clarity and maintainability without altering existing functionality.
The application continues to operate as expected after these changes.

prs.title: Update README.md
prs.body:
Header: A clear and attractive header with a background color and centered text.
Sections: Divided content into sections with appropriate headings for easy navigation.
Styling: Used simple CSS styles for colors, fonts, and layout, making it visually appealing without overwhelming complexity.
Code Blocks: Used <pre> tags to format code snippets nicely.

prs.title: Update README.md

Styling: Added CSS to enhance the visual appeal with colors, padding, and borders.
Structure: Organized sections clearly for easy reading.

Code Blocks: Improved the presentation of code snippets.

prs.title: Update README.md
prs.body:
Enhanced Aesthetics:

Added background colors, padding, and rounded corners to create a clean, modern look. Included box shadows for depth and separation from the background. Organized Structure:

Used a container to center the content and improve readability. Section headings are clearly defined with emojis to make them visually engaging. Improved Readability:

Used lists for features, structure, and instructions to enhance clarity. Included code blocks for commands to distinguish them from regular text. Links and Interactivity:

Hyperlinked relevant documents (CONTRIBUTING.md and LICENSE) to facilitate easy navigation. Added a mailto link for direct contact.
Responsive Design:

Used a responsive layout to ensure compatibility across devices, maintaining usability.

prs.title: Update README.md
prs.body:
1. Styling and Visual Appeal Color Scheme: Used a consistent color palette (e.g., tomato color for headings, Bootstrap blue for links) to create a visually appealing layout. Background Colors: Added background colors to different sections (FAQ, Legacy, Contributions) to make them stand out and improve readability.
2. Organization and Structure Headings and Subheadings: Organized content using clear headings (e.g., FAQ, Do, Don’t) to improve navigation and help users find information quickly. Bullet Points: Used lists for "Do" and "Don’t" sections to present information clearly and concisely.
3. Code Presentation Code Blocks: Utilized <code> and <pre> tags for code snippets, improving their visibility and making it clear what code contributions should look like. Syntax Highlighting: While not directly implemented in the HTML, using the <pre><code> structure sets the stage for potential syntax highlighting with CSS frameworks.
4. User Engagement Interactive Elements: Hyperlinked relevant sections (like the repository link) to encourage user interaction and exploration. Encouraging Tone: Maintained an encouraging and friendly tone throughout the content, which fosters a positive community spirit.
5. Accessibility and Readability Font and Line Spacing: Chose a clean font with adequate line spacing for better readability. Mobile Responsiveness: Set viewport meta tags to ensure the page is responsive on mobile devices.
6. Clarity and Consistency Consistent Terminology: Ensured consistent use of terms (e.g., “Pull Requests” consistently abbreviated as PRs) for clarity. Simplified Language: Used straightforward language to make the instructions easy to understand, especially for new contributors.

prs.title: Update README.md
prs.body:
1. Header Structure and Style Added a <header> tag: This provides semantic meaning and improves accessibility. Styled the header: I applied a background color and padding to make it visually distinct and appealing. It enhances the branding of the website.
2. Container for Main Content Wrapped the main content in a <div class="container">: This centers the content and provides a consistent layout with padding and a white background, making it easier to read against the gray background.
3. Font and Color Adjustments Set a default font-family and colors: This improves overall readability and provides a more modern look. Used color contrast for links: The blue link color enhances visibility and encourages interaction.
4. List and Preformatted Text Styles Styled <ul> and <ol>: Added margins and padding to ensure clear separation from other content. Formatted <pre> tags: Changed the background color and added padding for better readability of code snippets.
5. Footer Design Added a <footer> tag: This helps structure the page better and clearly delineates the footer content. Styled the footer: Similar to the header, I applied background color and padding to give it prominence and maintain consistency.
6. Content Organization Headings hierarchy: Ensured proper use of <h1>, <h2>, and <h3> tags to improve SEO and readability. Improved text clarity: Simplified some sentences and structured the content logically for better flow.
7. Responsive Design Considerations Used percentages for widths: Made images responsive by setting their max-width to 100%, ensuring they adapt to different screen sizes.
8. Links and Call-to-Actions Enhanced link visibility: Styled links to indicate interactivity with hover effects. Clear call-to-action: Ensured that links directing users to additional information were highlighted for easy access.
9. Code Block Formatting Improved formatting for code snippets: Added spaces and line breaks for better readability and clarity in instruction.

prs.title: Update login.html
prs.body:
1. Inline Styles for Improved Layout Added inline styles throughout the HTML to improve spacing, alignment, and overall aesthetics without requiring a separate CSS file.
2. Navigation Bar Enhancements Improved spacing and alignment in the navigation bar for better readability. Added hover effects to links for interactivity.
Styled buttons for "Sign Up" and "Login" to make them more visually appealing.
3. Hero Section Styling Applied a gradient background to the hero section for a modern look. Increased font sizes and adjusted padding for a more impactful introduction.
4. Course Recommendations Section Added a flexbox layout to display course cards responsively. Styled course cards with shadows, borders, and hover effects to create depth. Used consistent sizing for course thumbnails to enhance visual uniformity.
5. Contact Us Section Centered the contact form and adjusted padding for a clean layout. Added styling to input fields and the submit button for a cohesive design.
6. Footer Enhancements Structured the footer into two columns for important links and social media icons. Improved the appearance of footer links and social media icons for better accessibility and engagement.
7. Overall Aesthetic Improvements Used a cohesive color scheme throughout the page, ensuring consistency between sections. Enhanced typography with appropriate font sizes and weights to improve readability and hierarchy. These changes contribute to a more modern, elegant, and user-friendly interface, enhancing the overall user experience.

issues.title: Replace Google Form with an Integrated Web Form & Backend
issues.body:
Currently,  the websites has a Google Form embedded for collecting user responses. Instead of using Google Forms, we need to create a dedicated form on the website itself and implement a backend to handle and store the form data.
Expected Solution
Frontend:

Replace the Google Form with a custom-built form using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Ensure the form follows the current design theme of the website.
Include necessary fields that were present in the original Google Form.
Backend:

Create an API to handle form submissions.
Store the form data in a database (e.g., Firebase, Supabase, MongoDB, PostgreSQL).
Implement validation for form fields before storing data.
Deployment & Integration:

Ensure the form integrates smoothly with the existing website.
The backend should be hosted and accessible for handling submissions in real-time.

issues.title: 404 Error on Main Website Displaying Different Websites
issues.body: The main website, which is supposed to display different websites, is showing a 404 error. Additionally, some of the websites listed on the display are also showing a 404 error. This might be due to broken links or incorrect URL references. Possible Causes: The links to the websites might be incorrect or broken. The websites might not be deployed properly. A server or routing issue could be preventing access. The index.html file might not be properly configured or missing in some of the listed websites. Expected Behavior: The main website should correctly display the listed websites. This is the link of websites code [https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Qf2fcJjUW1TgDurgyozf3wfAP1OneGPp?usp=sharing](url)

issues.title: "Add Past Hackathon" Button Doesn't Persist Data
issues.body:
The 'Add Past Hackathon' button successfully takes user inputs and displays the output on the website. However, the data is not being saved persistently. When the page is refreshed, all added hackathons disappear.  

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Click on the "Add Past Hackathon" button.  
2. Enter the hackathon details (name, date, description, etc.).  
3. Submit the form and see the details appearing on the website.  
4. Refresh the page.  
5. The added hackathon disappears.  

Expected Behavior:
- The added hackathon details should be stored persistently (database, local storage, or backend).  
- After refreshing the page, the previously added hackathons should still be visible.  

Possible Causes:  
- The data might only be stored in the frontend state and not sent to a backend or saved in a database.  

Suggested Fix: 
Store the data persistently using one of the following approaches:  
  -Database (Recommended): Save the input in Firestore, Supabase, or another database.  
  - API Implementation: If using a backend, ensure the data is correctly sent and retrieved from the server.

issues.title: Display Live and Upcoming Hackathon Updates on the Website
issues.body: We need to implement a feature that dynamically displays live and upcoming hackathons on our website. The updates should be fetched in real-time or periodically through APIs or manually curated links. IT SHOULD BE UPDATED IN THIS DIV'S SHOWN IN IMAGE Expected Outcome: The website should display a continuously updated list of hackathons. Users should be able to see essential details at a glance and navigate to the registration pages easily. The feature should be automated as much as possible using APIs but allow manual updates as a backup.
